description: timing fot MAGAZINE Copyright 1917 by the New York Evening Post Company NEW YORK SATURDAY DECEMBER 1 1917 FREEDOM THAT ISNT FREEDOM Our old friend Freedom of the Seas recently qui escent German propaganda lias been otherwise busy is being groomed for another parade Before the procession starts let us remember that a German when he speaks of freedom of the seas does not mean what an American has in mind when he uses the phrase The historic American doctrine looks to the neutralization of the waterways of the world It contemplates such a recasting of international law as will make a neutral flagprotect both ship and cargo thus at one stroke ending the present law of contraband and the existing right of blockade This is freedom of the seas as we have preached it and those who do not take the trouble to make an examination assume that the Germans employ the phrase in the same sense That the Germans do not that they have in mind something entirely different sufficiently appears from the following signed by 352 German university profes sors CHRISTMAS NUMBER Drawn by Cesare Issued as Part 4 of The Evening Post every Saturday Price complete
